banner.title: Your "design (its words)" does not disappear — it is just <1>shared</1>
banner.sub: These are <1>words automatically read from the actual data</1> (across all datasets; names come from each dataset's design). The more they align, the better cross-dataset search and comparison work.

schemaError: Could not fetch the words of the actual data (the search backend is not responding).

classesCard.title: Shared words (actual data)
classesCard.note1: The number on the right = count in the actual data. <1>Kinds count how many things of that type exist</1>, <3>fields count how often they are used</3>.
classesCard.note2: * Only <1>published (citable) data</1> is counted. Unpublished drafts are not included — they enter the tally once published.
classesCard.reusesSubhead: Reused standard words (detected from the actual data)

usersCard.title: Datasets using these words
usersCard.empty: No ingested datasets yet. Once you save a design in the workbench and ingest it, it will appear here as a cross-dataset target.

caution.title: Why "handle with care"?
caution.body: Rewriting a shared word ripples into the search and answers of every dataset that uses it. Confirm changes after reviewing the <1>impact preview</1>.

known.qudt: QUDT (standard words for quantities and units)
known.cmso: CMSO (computational material samples — crystal structure, composition, defects)
